candidates.why: This is the only operating D&A row explicitly tied to COGS. However, it appears as a component beneath the selected parent row 'Cost of sales', with 'Cost of goods sold - Balancing value' indicating the parent already includes this amount, so adding it separately would double count.
Operating D&A row, but it belongs to SG&A rather than COGS, so it is not relevant for COGS_DA.
Operating D&A row, but it belongs to R&D rather than COGS, so it is not relevant for COGS_DA.

variables.notes: Selected COGS reference bucket is 'Cost of sales'. 'Depreciation/Amortization in COR/COGS' appears to be a sub-component of that selected row, not a separate same-level expense to add later.

candidates.why: This row appears twice and is tied to the selected SG&A component rows 'Selling costs' and 'Administrative expenses'. In both cases, adjacent balancing rows indicate the selected parent rows already include these D&A amounts, so they should not be added separately.

This is tied to 'Development costs', which is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD. Because the separate above-EBIT R&D row is already handled symmetrically in both buckets, adding R&D D&A separately would risk distortion/double counting.
Operating D&A row, but it belongs to COGS rather than SG&A/XSGA, so it is not relevant for XSGA_DA.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA reference bucket is the sum of 'Selling costs', 'Administrative expenses', and 'Development costs'. The visible SGA and R&D D&A rows appear as components within those selected rows rather than separate same-level expenses. No separate XSGA D&A row should be added.
